fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: District of Columbia Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to District of Columbia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity district of columbia, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to district of columbia, speech-language pathologist compact district of columbia, district of columbia speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Board of Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology
board.parent_dept: DC Department of Health (DOH), Health Regulation and Licensing Administration
board.mailing_address: 899 North Capitol St. NE, Washington, DC 20002
board.physical_address: 899 North Capitol St. NE, Washington, DC 20002

quick_facts.exam_name: National Examination in Speech-Language Pathology administered by Praxis

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in the District of Columbia.

license_types.title: Supervised Practice Registration
license_types.description: For SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship under supervision in DC.

steps.title: Complete Education Requirements
steps.description: Earn a graduate degree in speech-language pathology from an accredited educational institution.

steps.title: Complete Supervised Professional Experience
steps.description: Complete a supervised professional experience, as specified by the Board.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the National Examination in Speech-Language Pathology administered by Praxis.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: DC permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. DC is not currently a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. A valid DC SLP license is required to treat clients in the District.
provisional_text: DC offers a Supervised Practice Registration for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: DC does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full DC licensure.

faqs.question: How much does a DC SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost is approximately $187. Biennial renewal is $164.

faqs.question: Is DC part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: No. DC has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

faqs.question: Does DC allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. DC permits telepractice but requires a valid DC SLP license.

faqs.question: Does District of Columbia have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in District of Columbia. To work in District of Columbia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to District of Columbia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in District of Columbia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: DC does not participate in the ASLP Interstate Compact. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for full DC licensure.

hero.title: Become a Speech-Language Pathologist in District of Columbia
hero.dek: District of Columbia Speech-Language Pathologist licenses require a graduate degree, passing the national exam, and supervised professional experience.
